parsed_review.summary: CP00-243 is a clean descriptor-only closeout of the RP07.P02.M05 Permission And Audit Binding tail (5 rows) plus the RP07.P02.M06 Synthetic Fixture Set head (5 rows), 10 units total, risk class A. The pack-level descriptor production_ready_flag ("search_core_service_fixture_head_descriptor_verified") is supported consistently across implementation (registry.js PACK_BINDING/REQUIREMENTS, service.js freezeCp243Result + descriptor/case-set builders, validators.js 6 CP243 functions), tests (3 model.test.js cases asserting descriptor_only/runtime_execution:false, tenant/matter/permission prechecks, deny-over-allow, claude_is_final_approval:false), the contract (cp243_requirements + cp243_no_write_attestation + service_fixture_head_descriptor + historical binding tail), the validate-rp07 script, and the README ## CP00-243 section. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are fully intact: cp243_no_write_attestation holds every runtime/write/storage/permission/real-data flag false, including emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, loads_real_fixture_data:false, exposes_raw_payload:false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false, and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false. The manifest correctly leaves production_ready:false with status implemented_pending_review and a pending pack-level Claude review, so the downstream artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are expectedly absent rather than defects. Authority boundary preserved: Claude tools are restricted to Read/Grep/Glob and Claude is not promoted to final approver; Hermes appears only as deterministic control-plane evidence/review packet descriptors (H07.*/C07.*), not embedded product runtime. CP178+ review-hardening evidence is valid: readiness auth_preflight passed with 1445-byte non-empty stdout containing well-formed JSON ({"ok":true}), prompt is 2229B within the 25600B limit, forbidden_tools_present is false, source_inspection_mode is read_tools_used, and the contract forbidden_review_evidence list rejects failed-auth, zero-byte, malformed-JSON, fenced-JSON, tool-call-shaped output, claude_final_approval_claim and production_enterprise_trust_claim. All 19 expected validation commands report exit 0 with production_ready:false, no_real_data:true, writes_product_state:false. The changed-file scope (7 tracked modified files) matches the pack's implementation refs, and unrelated dirty/untracked files are correctly preserved as out of scope. Verdict is PASS_WITH_FINDINGS on a single P3 cosmetic note; nothing blocks pack or goal closeout, and human final approval remains required for any enterprise-trust claim.

findings.title: implementation_ref packages/search/src/index.js is unchanged in the working tree
findings.details: The manifest lists packages/search/src/index.js among the pack implementation_refs, but it is not among the 7 tracked modified files in changed-file-scope.json (its content is the three lines `export * from "./registry.js"; export * from "./service.js"; export * from "./validators.js";`). This is not a defect: because index.js re-exports each module via `export *`, the new CP243 public exports (createSearchCoreCp243ServiceFixtureHeadDescriptor, validateSearchCoreCp243ServiceFixtureHeadDescriptor, etc.) are surfaced transitively without any edit to index.js, so its inclusion as a public-surface implementation_ref is defensible. Noted only for transparency so the human adjudicator can confirm the unmodified-but-transitively-affected ref is intentional.
findings.recommendation: No change required. Optionally annotate in adjudication.md that index.js is an unmodified re-export aggregator whose public surface changes transitively via `export *`, to avoid a future reviewer mistaking the unchanged file for a missing implementation edit.
